info.title: CKSetup
info.description: Command line utility that setups (ie. run an Engine on) one or more binary paths: assemblies are unified and CKSetup.Runner is used to execute the specified Engine.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

commands.name: run
commands.description: Resolves Model/Setup dependencies across one or more directories, and runs a command in a consolidated folder.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Sets a verbosity level for console and/or file output. Valid verbosity levels: "Off", "Release", "Monitor", "Terse", "Verbose", "Debug", or any "{Group,Line}" format where Group and Line can be: "Debug", "Trace", "Info", "Warn", "Error", "Fatal", or "Off".

options.name: --logFile
options.description: Path of a log file which will contain the log output. Defaults to none (console logging only or determined by the Setup configuration).

options.name: --store
options.description:
Full path of the runtime store directory to use. Defaults to '%CKSETUP_STORE%' and is the result of the following process:
- The environment variable 'CKSETUP_STORE' like any other environment variables is expanded.
If the expansion of the environment variables of this string leads to a path with invalid characters in it this is an error,
but if the resuting path still contains a '%', this is just a warning (a referenced environment variable is missing).
- When the step above failed to resolve a path, a 'CKSetupStore.txt' file (that must contain the final store path)
is searched from the current directory up to the root. (Current directory is '<path>'.)
- When the step above failed to resolve a path, then the same lookup of a 'CKSetupStore.txt' file is done from the
current AppContext.BaseDirectory up to the root. (AppContext.BaseDirectory is '<path>'.)
- Ultimately, the shared local store is used (that is '<path>').

options.name: --remote
options.description: Url of the remote store. This uses the ambient store if it exists: use "no" or "none" to skip ambient store lookup.

options.name: --apiKey
options.description: Api key for the remote.

options.name: --launchDebug
options.description: Calls Debugger.Launch() at the very start of the runner process.

options.name: --force
options.description: Runs the full setup, regardless of the signature files that may exist in the configured BinPaths folders.

options.name: --forceEngine
options.description: Runs the engine, regardless of the signature files that may exist in the configured BinPaths folders.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.description: Path of a log file which will contain the log output. Defaults to none (console logging only or determined by the Setup configuration).

options.name: --prototype
options.description: Url of the prototype store. Use 'none' to remove current property store. Note that it can be a rooted file path to a local store.

options.name: --store
options.description:

- When the step above failed to resolve a path, then the same lookup of a 'CKSetupStore.txt' file is done from the
current AppContext.BaseDirectory up to the root. (AppContext.BaseDirectory is '<path>'.)
- Ultimately, the shared local store is used (that is '<path>').
